fields.helpText: This field contains the name of the site.
This field is used for the address of the Outpatient Site.
This field is used for the area code of the outpatient site.
This field is used for the telephone number of the outpatient site.
This field is used for the zip code of the outpatient site. This field will allow zip+4 format (excluding the "-").
This field is used to show the site/station number.
This field is used for the city in which the outpatient site is located.
This field is used to show the state in which the outpatient site resides.

This field will be printed on the laser labels mailing address label. It  can contain anything the site deems appropriate (i.e. whether mailing is  "Forwarding service requested" or "Address service requested", etc.)
This site parameter is used to determine if the 'Hold' function will be used at the medical facility.
This site parameter will be used to determine if the 'Suspense' feature will be used at the medical facility.
Prescriptions with duplicate classes can only be discontinued if this site parameter is set to 'Yes' and if the Rx has not been put on hold through CPRS.
This will be used to determine if inactive drugs will be used to refill active prescriptions.
This field will be used to determine if method of pickup will be asked for  window prescriptions.
This field is used to determine if pass medication within specified date range will be listed on profiles.
This field will be used to determine the sort order of medications on profiles.
This field will be used to determine the number copies for labels to print.
This field is used to determine if the duplicate drug warnings should be shown for non-pharmacist.
This field is used to determine if fee basis prescriptions are processed.
This field is used to determine if the multiple prescription request forms are printed with medication labels.
This field is used to determine if barcodes are printed on profiles,  labels, and multi request forms.

This field is used to indicate if barcodes are to print with the action profiles. Barcodes on action profiles are a new feature for version 6.0 of outpatient. The printer used must be setup or have barcode capabilities for the barcodes to print.  Contact IRM to help determine which printer have barcode capabilities.
This field is used to determine if prescriptions entered by a  non-pharmacist are placed in a non-verified status.
This field is used to determine which bingo board display screen will be shown on the waiting room monitor.
This field is used to determine if profiles are displayed when refilling and renewing medications.
This field is used to determine if editing of patient data will be allowed.
This field will be used to determine if drugs can be changed during the prescription edit option.
This field will be used to determine if renewing of medications will be allowed.
This field is used to determine if pass medications are to be cancelled.
This field is used to determine if medication that is refilled or renewed before the next possible fill date is to be placed  in suspense automatically.
This field is used to determine if the computer will auto generate prescription numbers.
This field is used to determine if medication profiles are printed when new medication is ordered.

This field will be used to allow printing of RX labels without being able to queue to a printer.  This parameter is primarily for slaved printing of RX labels.  If 'yes' is the answer the prompt for action to be taken on the label will include the '/PRINT' action.
This field will be used to determine if the site has a methadone program  and if a particular drug should be prompted for.
This field will be used to show what drug is being used if the site has a  methadone program.
THIS FIELD IS NO LONGER USED!  CLOZAPINE DATA ROLLUP HAS BEEN TAKEN OVER BY THE MENTAL HEALTH PACKAGE.   This field will be used to indicate the Drug Enforcement Agency ID  number for this site.
THIS FIELD IS NO LONGER USED!  CLOZAPINE DATA ROLLUP HAS BEEN TAKEN OVER BY THE MENTAL HEALTH PACKAGE.   This identifies the site by linking it to an entry in file 736.

fields.fieldName: DAYS TO PULL SUSP LOCAL NON-CS
DAYS TO PULL SUSP CMOP CS

fields.helpText: This field indicates the number of days a discontinued or expired med order appears in reverse video on a patient's med profile.

fields.fieldName: DAYS TO PULL SUSP LOCAL CS
DAYS TO PULL SUSP CMOP NON-CS

fields.helpText: This field will be used to determine which medication label stock will be used.

The entry in this field is used as a sites default response to the "Process telephone refills for all divisions?" prompt when running the  PROCESS TELEPHONE REFILLS option. If this field is set to Y the default prompt will be yes and N the default prompt will be NO.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:INTERFACE TURNED OFF;1:SEND ALL ORDERS AND PRINT LABEL;2:SEND ALL ORDERS AND DON'T PRINT LABEL;3:SEND MARKED ORDERS AND DON'T PRINT;4:SEND MARKED ORDERS AND PRINT LABEL;
fields.helpText: This field allows sites to alter the characteristics of the external interface. The Set of Codes field have the following values:   0 - the external interface is off 1 - send all drugs to the external interface; print labels locally 2 - send all drugs to the external interface; don't print labels locally 3 - send only marked drugs to the external interface; don't print labels     locally 4 - send only marked drugs to external interface and print labels through     VistA.
This field will determine if a blank label should print between patients on the label printers.

This site parameter will determine if the name of the verifying pharmacist  or the name of the person who made the order request will print on the Rx label.  If the parameter is set to Yes the verifying pharmacist name will print.
These are the Institutions of CPRS Pending orders that can be completed when signed in under this Outpatient site.
This is the default eRx clinic, used to hold the eRx clinic that will be  associated with an eRx prescription.
This field holds the number of days in the past the eRx software will  search the eRx holding queue on initial load, and when performing  searches.
Institution associated with the site.
The value in the field should point to the entry in the INSTITUTION file (#4) that contains the NPI number for the dispensing pharmacy for this Outpatient Pharmacy division.
This field will determine what version of the automated dispense machine  this site is running.  If the machine is older than HL7 V.2.4, enter  letter O, if HL7 V.2.4 has been installed, enter 2.4.
This field is used to indicate if the release date/time is to be filed for the prescription dispensed by an external interface.
This field will determine if the automated dispense machines are ready to  receive HL7 V.2.4 messages.
This field contains a pointer to the ScripTalk printer device in the  device file.

Set this to 'A' to have a ScripTalk label automatically print for  ScripTalk-enrolled patients whenever their regular medication label  prints. Set to 'M' if ScripTalk labels will be printed manually.

This field is used to determine the amount of readable prescription data that is printed on the ScripTalk labels that are read aloud by the ScripTalk Station Reader. Set this field according to the type of ScripTalk printer, either 2K or 10K. This setting is used to determine when to stop sending prescription data to the readable portion of the label and print a readable message to the patient to seek help for this particular prescription label, since there was not enough space for the prescript
This field allows the site to define, by division, whether or not they  wish to void the print of the drug name for the non-ScripTalk labels when  the patient is enrolled in ScripTalk.

Maximum number of label/profiles to be stored in the Label/Profile Monitor list for each printer device.  The default and minimum value for this number is 1000.  Each time the printer is used an entry is made in the Label/Profile monitor and if the monitor holds more entries than specified by this parameter, the oldest entry is deleted.

fields.fieldName: FDA MED GUIDE PRINT SERVER URL
fields.helpText: This is the Uniform Resource Locator(URL) and Port Number for the Print  Server running the Java Application responsible for automatically  printing of the Food and Drug Administration(FDA) Medication Guides for  the division (e.g., 'http://10.4.21.22:8092/').    If you do not want to use the FDA Medication Guide automatic printing  functionality do not populate this field.
This sub-file contains a list of printers used for printing FDA Medication Guides for this division.
This field is used to determine if narcotics will be numbered differently from other medications/supplies.
This field is used to determine what prescription numbers narcotics are to start with if narcotics are numbered differently from other  prescriptions/supplies.
This field is used to determine the highest prescription number used for narcotics if this type of medication is numbered differently from other medications/supplies.
This field is used to indicate last number issued for narcotic medications.
Select the appropriate entry in the Service/Section file (#49) that is to be used for the Pharmacy Copayment/Integrated Billing procedures.

This field contains information from the site regarding the copayment procedures for the patient to follow upon receipt of the copay document. For example, information may include a telephone number to call regarding billing inquiries, a payment mailing address, etc.

This field contains information from the site regarding procedures for the patient to obtain a refill of his refillable medication. May include the address, phone number, etc. that will assist the patient.

This field contains information from the site regarding procedures for the patient to obtain a new prescription for his non-refillable  medication. This field may contain address, phone number, etc that will assist the patient.

This field is a pointer to the Hospital Location File (#44). Multi-division sites should populate this field for each division. This data could be obtained from the Business Office of the Medical Center.
This field is the site-specific National Council for Prescription Drug  Programs number (NCPDP), formerly referred to as the National Association  of Boards of Pharmacy Number (NABP).
This field is used to enter the lowest prescription number for this site.
This field is used to enter the highest presciption number for this site.
This field is used to store the last RX number used.
This date will indicate that the Outpatient Site is no longer active, and cannot be selected through the Outpatient Pharmacy options.

This is a pointer to the Logical Link file (#870).  This link is used to transmit messages to an external (client) application.  This field is not filled in when messages are exchanged between DHCP applications on the same system. This field will be used as the Logical Link for the prescription if there is no Clinic associated with the prescription, or if there is a Clinic associated with the prescription, but the Institution derived from that Clinic does not have an entry in the CPRS ORDERING I
THIS IS THE DNS COMPUTER NAME OF THE AUTOMATED DISPENSING MACHINE THAT IS  USED FOR THIS OUTPATIENT SITE DIVISION.
ENTER THE DNS PORT NUMBER ASSOCIATED WITH THE AUTOMATED DISPENSE MACHINE  FOR THIS OUTPATIENT PHARMACY SITE DIVISION.
This field identifies the name of the dispensing system printer device. When the user selects this printer device and the external interface is in use, an HL7 message will be generated.

This flag should be set to Yes, only if the site wants the scheduled  AUTO REFILL [PSO AUTO REFILL] option to process refills for patients who are currently an inpatient. The AUTO REFILL job defaults to NO unless the site has this flagged as YES.

This flag should be set to Yes, only if the site wants the scheduled AUTO REFILL [PSO AUTO REFILL] option to process refills for CNH patients. The AUTO REFILL job defaults to NO unless the site has this flagged as YES.

This flag controls the functionality of the OneVA Pharmacy remote  prescription logic. If the flag is turned on, this facility will be able  to query prescriptions from other facilities, and will accept incoming  refill and partial fills from other VA medical facilities. If the flag is  turned 'off', no queries will be made, and external refill/partial fill  requests will be declined.
